content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 11, 1990


Rich Wiseman
P.O. Box 884
Ukiah, CA  95482

                                        Re: Your Request for Confirmation of
                                            Telephone Advice
                                            Our File No. I-90-221

Dear Mr. Wiseman:

     We have received your letter dated March 15, 1990 seeking
written confirmation of the telephone advice provided to you by
this agency on March 14, 1990 in response to your question
regarding your responsibilities under the provisions of the
Political Reform Act ("the Act"). Your question was whether, as
a lieutenant in the Mendocino County Sheriffs Department, you may
wear your uniform in a picture contained in a campaign brochure
for your campaign for Sheriff of Mendocino County.

     Your letter accurately summarizes the advice that you were
provided, to wit: there are no provisions of the Act or any
FPPC regulations that prohibited such activity on the part of a
candidate for elective office. Because the Commission does not
provide any advice concerning state statutes other than the Act,
you were advised to contact your county counsel for assistance
with any other provisions, such as county ordinances and
regulations. We are unable to confirm or deny the accuracy of
your assumption that, in the event a prohibition does exist, you
"have acted in good faith."

     I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this mat-
ter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: Jonathan S. Rothman
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh


_________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question: Whether, as a lieutenant in the Mendocino County Sheriff's Department, you may wear your uniform in a picture contained in a campaign brochure for your campaign for Sheriff of Mendocino County.
sections.conclusion: There are no provisions of the Act, or any FPPC Regulations, that prohibited such activity on the part of a candidate for elective office.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a candidate for Sheriff wear their official law enforcement uniform in campaign materials?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC found no provisions of the Political Reform Act or FPPC Regulations prohibiting a candidate from wearing their uniform in campaign materials, though the requestor was advised to consult county counsel regarding other applicable laws.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice. The document contains clear question and conclusion elements. Minor OCR artifacts present but do not substantially impact content extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether, as a lieutenant in the Mendocino County Sheriff's Department, you may wear your uniform in a picture contained in a campaign brochure for your campaign for Sheriff of Mendocino County.

CONCLUSION: There are no provisions of the Act, or any FPPC Regulations, that prohibited such activity on the part of a candidate for elective office.

Your letter accurately summarizes the advice that you were provided, to wit: there are no provisions of the Act or any FPPC regulations that prohibited such activity on the part of a candidate for elective office. embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms prior telephone advice that a lieutenant in the Mendocino County Sheriff's Department may wear his uniform in a campaign brochure for his Sheriff candidacy under the Political Reform Act, though other state and local laws should be reviewed.
